summary: Top signals include Anthropic's Claude Code making and containment practices, OpenAI's GPT-5.6 in GitHub Copilot, and new agent infrastructure like trigger.dev and microsoft/agent-framework. User workflow signals are missing from this shard. | This shard covers 47 discussion sources, revealing critical agent security threats (hallusquatting), an insider look at Claude Code development, an OpenAI product change (Atlas discontinued), and several user workflow reports. Sandboxing and MCP infrastructure are also actively debated.

candidates.title: The Making of Claude Code
candidates.why_it_matters: Insider story of how Claude Code evolved from internal CLI to Anthropic's coding agent
candidates.next_check: follow-up

candidates.title: Mitos – millisecond microVM sandbox forking
candidates.why_it_matters: Firecracker-based sandbox for AI agents on K8s, enabling fast fork and snapshot

candidates.title: Hol Guard – AI antivirus for developer agents
candidates.why_it_matters: Security tool to protect agents from malicious tools and MCP servers before execution

candidates.title: Attackers register fake packages AI agents hallucinate
candidates.why_it_matters: Critical user workflow: agents inventing package names that attackers squat, leading to malware infection.
candidates.next_check: Monitor for mitigation announcements from Cursor/Windsurf; verify if additional squatting vectors reported.

candidates.title: Anthropic publishes making-of Claude Code blog
candidates.why_it_matters: Official insider on how Anthropic built a new way to work with code.
candidates.next_check: Look for full blog post on Anthropic website; check for any new features announced.

candidates.title: OpenAI discontinues standalone ChatGPT Atlas; merges into new app
candidates.why_it_matters: Major product direction change: standalone browser agent (Atlas) killed, folded into ChatGPT app and Codex.
candidates.next_check: Monitor for user migration/feedback on the new app; check if Atlas features preserved.

gaps: Missing user_workflow: no operator field reports in this shard (social/discussion sources not included)
Missing social/discussion coverage: shard limited to official/repo sources
Missing mainstream_product: No Google, Microsoft, GitHub, Cursor, or Apple product changelog in this shard.
Missing user_workflow: Detailed field reports from Reddit or X (though Bluesky provided some); consider adding more direct user pain points.
No research papers or benchmarks in top candidates (though Sonnet 5 vs Opus 4.8 comparison exists as a secondary signal).
